text: "The sin of Phogor"... The sin committed in the worship of Beelphogor.

"Of children."... Women and children, ordinarily speaking, were not to be killed in war. (Deut. 20:14). But the great Lord of life and death was pleased to order it otherwise in the present case, in detestation of the wickedness of this people, who, by the counsel of Balaam, had sent their women amongst the Israelites on purpose to draw them from God.
